rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order 1) Approving Receiver Final Account And Report, 2) Approving Receiver Fees And Expenses, 3) Approving Distribution Of Funds, 4) Discharging Receiver, 5) Exonerating Bond, 6) Requiring Court Permission And Funding The Receivership In Order To File Claim That Involves Receiver, 7) Retaining Jurisdiction Re Matters Or Claims Involving Receiver, 8) Authorizing Plaintiff To Obtain Residual Proceeds From Case Cgc 18-564941; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Kevin Whelan In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 21, 2022 line 1. OTHER KEVIN WHELAN MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order 1) Approving Receiver Final Account And Report, 2) Approving Receiver Fees And Expenses, 3) Approving Distribution Of Funds, 4) Discharging Receiver, 5) Exonerating Bond, 6) Requiring Court Permission And Funding The Receivership In Order To File Claim That Involves Receiver, 7) Retaining Jurisdiction Re Matters Or Claims Involving Receiver, 8) Authorizing Plaintiff To Obtain Residual Proceeds From Case Cgc 18-564941; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Kevin Whelan In Support Thereof is GRANTED as proposed by the receiver, except for the distribution to the Plaintiff. Distribution to the Plaintiffs at this time shall be limited to the remainder of funds in the estate after $500,000 is transferred to the receiver, Kevin Singer in Umpqua action, to be held in reserve to cover Umpqua's incurred and potential fees to litigate 5 outstanding appeals and outstanding trial level attorney's fees previously awarded by the Court. The Court makes no determination as to the final amount of fees or entitlement to fees on appeal at this time. This issue will be addressed upon the conclusion of the appeals and any interested parties will be entitled to submit their claims to the reserve funds thereafter. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: COLBY LAM VS. ANDREW KENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 21, 2022 line 2. CROSS COMPLAINANT ANDREW KENNY, GERALDINE QUINEY Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs is GRANTED. The Court will consider this motion as a request for relief under mandatory provision of CCP 473(b). Counsel for the moving party admits to providing an incorrect service address which ultimately resulted in failure to file a timely motion to tax/strike. M-J SF Investments shall recover $630.50 in filing fees, exhibit costs and e-filing costs. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
